frame_analyses.description: FLS trainer setup visible with Penrose drain secured by clamps, marks visible on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard task setup, trainer positioned appropriately

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver entering field and approaching needle on gauze pad
Right instrument grasping needle at approximately 2/3 distance from tip
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grip position, proper orientation for suturing

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver positioning needle for entry through first mark on Penrose drain
Needle being driven through drain at first mark, left instrument providing counter-traction
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bimanual coordination, appropriate depth of bite

frame_analyses.description: Needle emerging through drain, left instrument assisting with needle retrieval
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle passage, good instrument coordination

frame_analyses.description: Left needle driver grasping needle tip for hand-off from right instrument
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth instrument exchange, maintaining needle control

frame_analyses.description: Left instrument positioning needle for entry at second mark
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good positioning for second entry point

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through second mark on drain
Completion of suture placement through both marks, preparing for knot tying
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both entry points appear accurate, good suture positioning

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle to begin first throw formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initiating surgeon's knot with right hand leading

frame_analyses.description: Double throw surgeon's knot formation in progress
Completing first surgeon's knot throw with appropriate tension
Left instrument now leading for second throw formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate hand switching from right to left

frame_analyses.description: Right instrument leading third throw, completing knot sequence
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand alternation maintained, final throw formation

frame_analyses.description: Scissors entering field to cut suture tails

penalties.description: Entry point at first mark appears to be approximately 1mm medial to center

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw formation with appropriate tension
Proper hand switch maintained, good throw formation
Final throw with appropriate hand alternation

confidence_rationale: Clear view of most key phases including knot formation and hand switching. Some uncertainty in precise measurement of suture deviation due to camera angle and resolution. Completion time and major technique elements well-observed.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation measurements due to camera angle
Whether suture tails were cut to appropriate length inside trainer
technique_summary: Excellent performance with expert-level completion time and proper technique. Clean suture placement, appropriate knot formation with correct hand switching sequence, and efficient execution throughout.
strengths: Excellent overall completion time of 88 seconds
Proper hand switching maintained throughout knot sequence
Smooth instrument exchanges and bimanual coordination
Clean needle loading and suture placement technique
Secure knot formation with appropriate tension
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle entry at marked points to minimize deviation penalties
Consider slightly slower approach to suture placement to ensure perfect accuracy at marks
